Subsection.Text: An external adjudicator shall be paid the remuneration and the fees that may be fixed by the Chairperson and is entitled to be paid reasonable travel and living expenses incurred by them in the course of their duties while absent from their ordinary place of residence.

Section.Text: In dealing with any complaint under this Part, the Board

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: shall proceed as informally and expeditiously as the circumstances and considerations of fairness permit; and

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: is not bound by legal or technical rules of evidence.

Section.Text: The Board has, in relation to a complaint referred to it by the Commissioner, the power

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: to summon and enforce the attendance of witnesses and compel them to give oral or written evidence on oath and to produce the documents and things that the Board considers necessary, in the same manner and to the same extent as a superior court of record;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: to administer oaths;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: to receive, and base a decision on, any evidence adduced that the Board believes to be credible;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: to compel any person to provide information or produce documents and things that may be relevant to a matter before the Board, after providing the parties with the opportunity to make representations;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: subject to any limitations prescribed by regulation of the Governor in Council, to enter any premises of a federally regulated private business that is the subject of a complaint and to inspect and view anything found in the premises that may be relevant to the complaint and require any person to answer any question that may be relevant to the complaint;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: to abridge or extend the time for doing any act, filing any document or presenting any evidence;

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: if the parties agree, to assist the parties in resolving any issues in dispute by any means that the Board considers appropriate, without prejudice to the Boardâs power to determine issues that have not been settled;

Paragraph.Label: (h)
Paragraph.Text: to authorize any person to do anything that the Board may do under paragraphs (a) to (g) and to report to the Board on it;

Paragraph.Label: (i)
Paragraph.Text: to adjourn or postpone any proceeding from time to time;

Paragraph.Label: (j)
Paragraph.Text: to defer deciding any matter, if the Board considers that the matter could be resolved by an alternate method of resolution;

Paragraph.Label: (k)
Paragraph.Text: to amend or permit the amendment of any document filed in connection with the complaint;

Paragraph.Label: (l)
Paragraph.Text: to add a party at any stage;

Paragraph.Label: (m)
Paragraph.Text: to permit an interested person to intervene at any stage;

Paragraph.Label: (n)
Paragraph.Text: to merge complaints that relate to the same situation or subject matter;

Paragraph.Label: (o)
Paragraph.Text: to decide any matter that may arise in connection with the complaint;

Paragraph.Label: (p)
Paragraph.Text: to take notice of facts that may be judicially noticed;

Paragraph.Label: (q)
Paragraph.Text: to take notice of other generally recognized facts and any information that is within the Boardâs specialized knowledge, after notifying the parties and any intervenor of its intention to do so and providing them with an opportunity to make representations; and

Paragraph.Label: (r)
Paragraph.Text: to review, rescind, amend, alter or vary any order or decision made by the Board, and to rehear any matter before making a decision with respect to it.

Section.Text: A member of the Board or an external adjudicator may, in respect of any complaint referred to the Board, consult with any member of the Board or with any employee of the Administrative Tribunals Support Service of Canada.

Subsection.Text: The Board may make regulations respecting its powers, duties and functions under this Part, including regulations respecting

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: rules of procedure for proceedings;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the use of means of telecommunication that permit simultaneous communication;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: the forms to be used in connection with a complaint;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the time within which and the circumstances under which the Board may exercise its powers under this Part;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: the form in which and the period during which evidence may be presented to the Board;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: the time within which and the parties or persons to whom notices and other documents shall be sent and the circumstances in which the notices or documents are deemed to have been given or received by the Board or any party or person; and

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: the delegation of the Boardâs powers under paragraph 81.8(h).

Subsection.Text: Section 57 does not apply with respect to regulations made under subsection (1).
The Board may reject a complaint, in whole or in part, if the Board is satisfied that

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: the complaint is not within its jurisdiction;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: the complaint is frivolous, vexatious or not made in good faith;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: there is insufficient evidence to substantiate the complaint;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: the complaint has been settled in writing between the complainant and the federally regulated private business;

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: there are other means available to the complainant to resolve the subject matter of the complaint that the Board considers should be pursued;

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: the subject matter of the complaint has been adequately dealt with through recourse obtained before a court, tribunal, arbitrator or adjudicator; or

Paragraph.Label: (g)
Paragraph.Text: if the complainant is subject to a collective agreement, the collective agreement covers the subject matter of the complaint and provides a third party dispute resolution process.

Subsection.Text: If the Board rejects a complaint, it shall notify the parties in writing, with reasons.

Section.Text: If the Board decides that a complaint is well-founded, the Board may, by order, require the federally regulated private business that is the subject of the complaint to comply with the section of this Act at issue and, if applicable, to

Paragraph.Label: (a)
Paragraph.Text: permit the complainant to return to the duties of their employment;

Paragraph.Label: (b)
Paragraph.Text: reinstate the complainant;

Paragraph.Label: (c)
Paragraph.Text: pay to the complainant compensation not exceeding the sum that, in the Boardâs opinion, is equivalent to the remuneration that would, but for the failure to comply, have been paid to the complainant;

Paragraph.Label: (d)
Paragraph.Text: pay to the complainant compensation not exceeding the sum that, in the Boardâs opinion, is equivalent to any financial or other penalty imposed on the complainant by the federally regulated private business; and

Paragraph.Label: (e)
Paragraph.Text: do any other thing that the Board considers equitable for the federally regulated private business to do to remedy or counteract any consequence of the failure to comply.

Section.Text: The Board shall provide all parties, as well as the Commissioner, with a copy of its decision on whether a complaint is well-founded and of any related order made under section 81.93, with reasons.

Subsection.Text: Any person affected by an order of the Board made under section 81.93, or the Commissioner on the request of such a person, may file a certified copy of the order, exclusive of reasons, in the Federal Court of Appeal after the later of 14 days after the day on which the order is made and 14 days after a day that may be provided for in the order.

Once filed, the order shall be registered in the Court and, when registered, has the same force and effect, and all proceedings may be taken in respect of it, as if the order were a judgment obtained in that Court.

Section.Text: No civil remedy of an employee against an employer is suspended or affected by this Part.
The Governor in Council may make regulations for the purposes of this Part.
Section 83 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Nothing in this Act abrogates or derogates from any legal or customary right acquired or enjoyed either before or after the coming into force of this Act with respect to any language other than English or French, including any Indigenous language.

Nothing in this Act shall be interpreted in a manner that is inconsistent with the maintenance and enhancement of languages other than English or French, nor with the reclamation, revitalization and strengthening of Indigenous languages.

Section.Text: Subsection 86(3) of the English version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: In calculating the 30-day period referred to in subsection (1), only the days on which both Houses of Parliament sit shall be counted.

Section.Text: Subsection 87(5) of the French version of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: Pour lâapplication du prÃ©sent article, jour de sÃ©ance sâentend, Ã lâÃ©gard dâune chambre du Parlement, de tout jour oÃ¹ elle siÃ¨ge.

Section.Text: Section 89 of the Act is replaced by the following:
Section 126 of the Criminal Code does not apply to or in respect of any contravention of any provision of this Act or the regulations.
Section 91 of the Act is replaced by the following:

Nothing in this Act authorizes the application of official language requirements to a particular staffing action unless those requirements are objectively required to perform the functions for which the staffing action is undertaken.
The Act is amended by adding the following after section 93:

Subsection.Text: On the 10th anniversary of the day on which this section comes into force and every 10 years after that anniversary, the Minister of Canadian Heritage shall undertake a review of the provisions and operation of this Act.
That Minister shall cause a report of the review to be tabled in each House of Parliament within the first 30 days on which that House is sitting after the report has been completed.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after the heading âTransitionalâ after the heading of Part XIV:

Section.MarginalNote: Compliance agreements â Quebec (communications and services)

Subsection.Text: The Commissioner is not permitted to exercise, before the day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council, the powers under subsection 64.1(1) in respect of a complaint made by a consumer in Quebec in respect of a right or duty under section 45.21.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Compliance agreements â Quebec (language of work)
Subsection.Text: The Commissioner is not permitted to exercise, before the day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council, the powers under subsection 64.1(1) in respect of a complaint made by an employee who occupies or is assigned to a position in a workplace in Quebec in respect of a right or duty under any of sections 45.22 to 45.24.

The Commissioner is not permitted to exercise, before the day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council, the powers under subsection 64.5(1) in respect of a complaint made by a consumer in Quebec in respect of a right or duty under section 45.21.

The Commissioner is not permitted to exercise, before the day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council, the powers under subsection 64.5(1) in respect of a complaint made by an employee who occupies or is assigned to a position in a workplace in Quebec in respect of a right or duty under any of sections 45.22 to 45.24.

Section.Text: The Act is amended by adding the following after section 104:

Section.MarginalNote: Compliance agreements â regions with a strong francophone presence (communications and services)

Subsection.Text: The Commissioner is not permitted to exercise, before the day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council, the powers under subsection 64.1(1) in respect of a complaint made by a consumer in a region with a strong francophone presence in respect of a right or duty under section 45.21.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Compliance agreements â regions with a strong francophone presence (language of work)
Subsection.Text: The Commissioner is not permitted to exercise, before the day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council, the powers under subsection 64.1(1) in respect of a complaint made by an employee who occupies or is assigned to a position in a workplace in a region with a strong francophone presence in respect of a right or duty under any of sections 45.22 to 45.24.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Orders â regions with a strong francophone presence (communications and services)
Subsection.Text: The Commissioner is not permitted to exercise, before the day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council, the powers under subsection 64.5(1) in respect of a complaint made by a consumer in a region with a strong francophone presence in respect of a right or duty under section 45.21.

Subsection.MarginalNote: Orders â regions with a strong francophone presence (language of work)
Subsection.Text: The Commissioner is not permitted to exercise, before the day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council, the powers under subsection 64.5(1) in respect of a complaint made by an employee who occupies or is assigned to a position in a workplace in a region with a strong francophone presence in respect of a right or duty under any of sections 45.22 to 45.24.

Section.Text: Sections 107 and 108 of the Act are repealed.

Subsection 9(2) of the Canada Labour Code is amended by striking out âandâ at the end of paragraph (d), by adding âandâ at the end of paragraph (e) and by adding the following after paragraph (e):

Paragraph.Label: (f)
Paragraph.Text: any other full-time or part-time members that the Governor in Council considers necessary to assist the Board in carrying out its functions under the Official Languages Act.

Section.Text: Subsection 10(3) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The members of the Board appointed pursuant to paragraph 9(2)(e) or (f) are to be appointed by the Governor in Council, on the recommendation of the Minister, to hold office during good behaviour for terms not exceeding three years each, subject to removal by the Governor in Council at any time for cause.
The members of the Board appointed pursuant to paragraph 9(2)(f) must have experience and expertise in official language rights.

Section.Text: Subsection 11(2) of the Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: A part-time Vice-Chairperson, or a member appointed pursuant to paragraph 9(2)(e) or (f), must not hold any other employment or office in respect of which they receive any remuneration and that is inconsistent with their duties under this Act or the Official Languages Act.

Section.Text: Section 12.02 of the Act is amended by adding the following after subsection (4):

Subsection.Text: For greater certainty, members appointed pursuant to paragraph 9(2)(f) are not permitted to vote on the making of regulations under section 15.

Section.Text: The Department of Canadian Heritage Act is amended by adding the following after section 7:

To promote a greater understanding of human rights, fundamental freedoms and related values, the Minister may take measures to provide funding to an organization, independent of the Government of Canada, responsible for administering a program whose purpose is to provide funding for test cases of national significance to be brought before the courts to clarify and assert constitutional human rights.
Subsection 10(1) of the Air Canada Public Participation Act is replaced by the following:

Subsection.Text: The Official Languages Act, other than Part VII.1, applies to the Corporation.

Section.Text: Section 15 of the CN Commercialization Act is replaced by the following:
The Official Languages Act, other than Part VII.1, continues to apply to CN as if it continued to be a federal institution within the meaning of that Act.

TitleText.XRefExternal: Civil Air Navigation Services Commercialization Act

Section.Text: Section 96 of the Civil Air Navigation Services Commercialization Act is replaced by the following:
The Official Languages Act, other than Part VII.1, applies to the Corporation as if it were a federal institution.
The Governor in Council may, by regulation, repeal the C.N.R. Company Exemption Order, C.R.C., c. 1244.

If Bill C-10, introduced in the 2nd session of the 43rd Parliament and entitled An Act to amend the Broadcasting Act and to make related and consequential amendments to other Acts, receives royal assent, then, on the first day on which both section 25 of that Act and section 22 of this Act are in force, section 42.1 of the Official Languages Act is replaced by the following:

The Government of Canada recognizes that the Canadian Broadcasting Corporation, in carrying out its purposes under the Broadcasting Act and subject to any applicable orders and regulations of the Canadian Radio-television and Telecommunications Commission, contributes through its activities to enhancing the vitality of the English and French linguistic minority communities in Canada and to the protection and promotion of both official languages. This recognition is made while respecting the freedom of expression and the journalistic, creative and programming independence enjoyed by the Canadian Broadcasting Corporation.

Subsection.Text: Subsections 2(4), 3(2), 6(3) and 23(2), sections 26, 37 and 45, subsection 46(1) and sections 50, 57, 60 to 63 and 65 to 67 come into force on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.
Subsections 2(5) and 3(3) and sections 27 to 32 and 58 come into force on the second anniversary of the day on which section 26 comes into force.
Section 24 comes into force on a day to be fixed by order of the Governor in Council.
